state.output:
<path>/app/mise.toml</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # https://mise.jdx.dev
2: # Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
3: # repository tasks.
4: 
5: experimental_monorepo_root = true
6: 
7: [settings]
8: lockfile = true
9: lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
10: pin = true
11: experimental = true
12: python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
13: windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
14: windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
15: unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
16: unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
17: # On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
18: # mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
19: activate_aggressive = true
20: 
21: [monorepo]
22: config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]
23: 
24: [tools]
25: # General tooling
26: "aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks
27: "aqua:jdx/hk" = "1.45.0" # Linter orchestration
28: "aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency
29: "aqua:dprint/dprint" = "0.53.2" # Formatter orchestration
30: "github:sargunv/jvl" = "2026.05.03" # JSON validator
31: "aqua:rhysd/actionlint" = "1.7.8" # GH Actions linter
32: "aqua:koalaman/shellcheck" = "0.11.0" # Shell script linter (used by actionlint)
33: 
34: # C/C++ ecosystem
35: "github:prefix-dev/pixi" = "0.68.1"
36: "aqua:Kitware/CMake" = "4.3.3"
37: "aqua:ninja-build/ninja" = "1.13.2"
38: "conda:clang-format" = "22.1.7"
39: "conda:clang-tools" = "22.1.7"
40: "conda:doxygen" = "1.13.2"
41: "conda:glslang" = "16.3.0"
42: "conda:pkg-config" = "0.29.2"
43: "conda:vulkan-tools" = "1.4.341.0"
44: "github:cmakefmt/cmakefmt" = "1.3.0"
45: "aqua:XcodesOrg/xcodes" = { version = "1.6.2", os = ["macos"] }
46: 
47: # Node.js ecosystem
48: "core:node" = "24.11.0"
49: "aqua:pnpm/pnpm" = "10.33.2"
50: 
51: # Java/Kotlin ecosystem
52: "core:java" = "temurin-25.0.3+9.0.LTS"
53: "aqua:facebook/ktfmt" = "0.62"
54: "github:google/google-java-format" = "1.35.0"
55: 
56: # Python ecosystem
57: "core:python" = "3.14.4"
58: "aqua:astral-sh/uv" = "0.11.11"
59: 
60: # Swift ecosystem
61: "core:swift" = { version = "6.3.1", os = ["macos"] }
62: "github:nicklockwood/SwiftFormat" = "0.61.1"
63: 
64: # Other ecosystems
65: "core:rust" = { version = "1.95.0", profile = "minimal", components = "rustfmt,clippy" }
66: "core:zig" = "0.16.0"
67: "core:go" = "1.26.3"
68: "core:dotnet" = "10.0.203"
69: 
70: [env]
71: UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
72: MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
73: MLN_FFI_VARIANT = { required = true }
74: MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = { required = true }
75: MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = { required = true }
76: MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR = { required = true }
77: MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE = "{{env.MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE | default(value=\"RelWithDebInfo\")}}"
78: MLN_FFI_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/include"
79: MLN_FFI_VULKAN_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/third_party/maplibre-native/vendor/Vulkan-Headers/include"
80: MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/include"
81: MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"
82: MLN_FFI_ZIG_TARGET = { required = true }
83: MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS = { required = true }
84: MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT = "{{env.MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT | default(value=\"30s\")}}"
85: CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default"
86: PKG_CONFIG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib/pkgconfig"
87: LIBCLANG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"
88: 
89: [hooks]
90: postinstall = [
91:   { task = "sync-submodules" },
92:   "hk install --mise --quiet",
93:   "pixi install --locked --quiet",
94:   # conda-forge ships libclang-13.dll, but clang-sys on Windows searches for libclang.dll.
95:   # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213
96:   # https://github.com/conda-forge/llvmdev-feedstock/issues/376
97:   '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
98:   "uv sync --locked --quiet",
99:   "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
100:   "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
101:   "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
102:   # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
103:   "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
104: ]
105: 
106: [tasks.configure]
107: depends = ["//:deps"]
108: run = [
109:   '''
110:   cmake_args=(
111:     -S .
112:     -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
113:     -G Ninja
114:     -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
115:     -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
116:     -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
117:     -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
118:   )
119: 
120:   while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
121:     [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
122:   done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"
123: 
124:   cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
125:   ''',
126:   '''
127:   if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
128:     mkdir -p build/clangd
129:     compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
130:     compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
131:     rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
132:     ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
133:     mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
134:   fi
135:   ''',
136: ]
137: 
138: [tasks.build]
139: depends = ["//:configure"]
140: run = '''
141: build_args=(
142:   --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
143:   --parallel
144: )
145: 
146: cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''
147: 
148: [tasks.clean]
149: run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""
150: 
151: [tasks.deps]
152: description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
153: run = '''
154: if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
155:   mise run deps:angle
156: fi
157: '''
158: 
159: [tasks.test]
160: depends = ["//:build"]
161: run = '''
162: (
163:   cd src/c_api/tests/zig
164:   zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
165: )
166: (
167:   cd bindings/zig
168:   zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
169: )
170: '''
171: 
172: [tasks.check]
173: run = "hk check --all"
174: 
175: [tasks.fix]
176: run = "hk fix --all"
177: 
178: [tasks.actions-up]
179: description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
180: run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"
181: 
182: [tasks._ktfmt]
183: description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
184: hide = true
185: run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
186: # ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
187: shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'
188: 
189: [tasks."ci:lint"]
190: run = [
191:   # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
192:   "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
193:   "hk fix --all",
194:   "git update-index -q --refresh",
195:   # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
196:   "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
197:   "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
198: ]
199: 
200: [tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
201: description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
202: run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

(End of file - total 202 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

display.text:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

[monorepo]
config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]

[tools]
# General tooling
"aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks
"aqua:jdx/hk" = "1.45.0" # Linter orchestration
"aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency
"aqua:dprint/dprint" = "0.53.2" # Formatter orchestration
"github:sargunv/jvl" = "2026.05.03" # JSON validator
"aqua:rhysd/actionlint" = "1.7.8" # GH Actions linter
"aqua:koalaman/shellcheck" = "0.11.0" # Shell script linter (used by actionlint)

# C/C++ ecosystem
"github:prefix-dev/pixi" = "0.68.1"
"aqua:Kitware/CMake" = "4.3.3"
"aqua:ninja-build/ninja" = "1.13.2"
"conda:clang-format" = "22.1.7"
"conda:clang-tools" = "22.1.7"
"conda:doxygen" = "1.13.2"
"conda:glslang" = "16.3.0"
"conda:pkg-config" = "0.29.2"
"conda:vulkan-tools" = "1.4.341.0"
"github:cmakefmt/cmakefmt" = "1.3.0"
"aqua:XcodesOrg/xcodes" = { version = "1.6.2", os = ["macos"] }

# Node.js ecosystem
"core:node" = "24.11.0"
"aqua:pnpm/pnpm" = "10.33.2"

# Java/Kotlin ecosystem
"core:java" = "temurin-25.0.3+9.0.LTS"
"aqua:facebook/ktfmt" = "0.62"
"github:google/google-java-format" = "1.35.0"

# Python ecosystem
"core:python" = "3.14.4"
"aqua:astral-sh/uv" = "0.11.11"

# Swift ecosystem
"core:swift" = { version = "6.3.1", os = ["macos"] }
"github:nicklockwood/SwiftFormat" = "0.61.1"

# Other ecosystems
"core:rust" = { version = "1.95.0", profile = "minimal", components = "rustfmt,clippy" }
"core:zig" = "0.16.0"
"core:go" = "1.26.3"
"core:dotnet" = "10.0.203"

[env]
UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
MLN_FFI_VARIANT = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE = "{{env.MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE | default(value=\"RelWithDebInfo\")}}"
MLN_FFI_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/include"
MLN_FFI_VULKAN_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/third_party/maplibre-native/vendor/Vulkan-Headers/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"
MLN_FFI_ZIG_TARGET = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT = "{{env.MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT | default(value=\"30s\")}}"
CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default"
PKG_CONFIG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib/pkgconfig"
LIBCLANG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"

[hooks]
postinstall = [
  { task = "sync-submodules" },
  "hk install --mise --quiet",
  "pixi install --locked --quiet",
  # conda-forge ships libclang-13.dll, but clang-sys on Windows searches for libclang.dll.
  # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/llvmdev-feedstock/issues/376
  '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
  "uv sync --locked --quiet",
  "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
  "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
]

[tasks.configure]
depends = ["//:deps"]
run = [
  '''
  cmake_args=(
    -S .
    -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
    -G Ninja
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
    -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
    -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
    -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
  )

  while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
    [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
  done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"

  cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
  ''',
  '''
  if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
    mkdir -p build/clangd
    compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
    compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
    rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
    ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
    mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
  fi
  ''',
]

[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:configure"]
run = '''
build_args=(
  --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
  --parallel
)

cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''

[tasks.clean]
run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""

[tasks.deps]
description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
run = '''
if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
  mise run deps:angle
fi
'''

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:build"]
run = '''
(
  cd src/c_api/tests/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
(
  cd bindings/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
'''

[tasks.check]
run = "hk check --all"

[tasks.fix]
run = "hk fix --all"

[tasks.actions-up]
description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"

[tasks._ktfmt]
description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
hide = true
run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
# ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'

[tasks."ci:lint"]
run = [
  # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "git update-index -q --refresh",
  # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
  "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
  "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
]

[tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

state.title: mise.toml

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30

display.text:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal' }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

state.title: .github/workflows/ci.yml

part.text: Now let me understand the variant env definitions. The mise envs listed in AGENTS.md: `linux-x64-vulkan`, `linux-x64-egl`, `linux-arm64-vulkan`, `linux-arm64-egl`, `macos-arm64-metal`, `macos-arm64-vulkan`, `macos-arm64-egl`, `windows-x64-vulkan`, `windows-x64-wgl`. But the CI matrix also includes `ios-arm64-metal` and `ios-simulator-arm64-metal`. Let me look at .mise directory for env definitions.

MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = "macos-arm64"
MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = "metal"

MLN_FFI_SYSTEM_ROOT = "{% if os() == \"macos\" %}{% if env.SDKROOT is defined and env.SDKROOT != \"\" %}{{ env.SDKROOT }}{% else %}{{ exec(command='xcrun --sdk macosx --show-sdk-path', cache_key='macosx-sdk-path', cache_duration='1d') | trim }}{% endif %}{% endif %}"
JDK_JAVA_OPTIONS = "-Djava.library.path={{env.MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR}} -Dorg.lwjgl.vulkan.libname=libvulkan.1.dylib"
VK_ICD_FILENAMES = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/share/vulkan/icd.d/MoltenVK_icd.json"
RUSTFLAGS = "-C link-arg=-Wl,-rpath,{{env.MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR}} -C link-arg=-Wl,-rpath,{{env.MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR}}"

<content>
1: name: Setup CI deps
2: description: Install sccache and mise toolchain
3: 
4: runs:
5:   using: composite
6:   steps:
7:     - name: Enable Git long paths
8:       if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
9:       shell: pwsh
10:       run: git config --global core.longpaths true
11: 
12:     - name: Cache MapLibre Native source
13:       uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
14:       with:
15:         path: |
16:           third_party/maplibre-native
17:           .git/modules/third_party/maplibre-native
18:         key: mln-src-v2-${{ hashFiles('.gitmodules', 'cmake/mln_version.cmake') }}
19: 
20:     - name: Install sccache
21:       uses: mozilla-actions/sccache-action@9e7fa8a12102821edf02ca5dbea1acd0f89a2696 # v0.0.10
22: 
23:     - name: Use Linux software graphics drivers
24:       if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
25:       shell: bash
26:       run: |
27:         sudo apt-get update
28:         sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes \
29:           libegl-mesa0 \
30:           libgl1-mesa-dri \
31:           libvulkan1 \
32:           mesa-utils \
33:           mesa-utils-extra \
34:           mesa-vulkan-drivers
35:         {
36:           echo "EGL_PLATFORM=surfaceless"
37:           echo "LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=true"
38:         } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
39: 
40:     - name: Use Windows software graphics drivers
41:       if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
42:       shell: pwsh
43:       run: |
44:         $mesaVersion = "26.0.8"
45:         $mesaArchive = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d.7z"
46:         $mesaRoot = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d"
47:         $mesaUrl = "https://github.com/pal1000/mesa-dist-win/releases/download/$mesaVersion/mesa3d-$mesaVersion-release-msvc.7z"
48: 
49:         Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $mesaUrl -OutFile $mesaArchive
50:         $expectedHash = "A438C26C2752726916E455F9AD121F8A7E3CFECF8626251ABF8E5B3E129D8497"
51:         $actualHash = (Get-FileHash $mesaArchive -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
52:         if ($actualHash -ne $expectedHash) {
53:           throw "Mesa archive hash mismatch: expected $expectedHash, got $actualHash"
54:         }
55:         7z x $mesaArchive "-o$mesaRoot" -y | Out-Null
56: 
57:         $mesaWglDriver = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "libgallium_wgl.dll" |
58:           Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "x64" } |
59:           Select-Object -First 1
60:         if (-not $mesaWglDriver) {
61:           throw "Missing Mesa WGL driver in $mesaArchive"
62:         }
63: 
64:         $driverManifest = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "lvp_icd.x86_64.json" |
65:           Select-Object -First 1
66:         if (-not $driverManifest) {
67:           throw "Missing lavapipe Vulkan ICD manifest in $mesaArchive"
68:         }
69: 
70:         $mesaBin = $mesaWglDriver.Directory.FullName
71:         $mesaWglSystemDriver = Join-Path $env:WINDIR "System32\mesadrv.dll"
72:         Copy-Item $mesaWglDriver.FullName $mesaWglSystemDriver -Force
73: 
74:         $openglDriverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\OpenGLDrivers\MSOGL"
75:         New-Item -Path $openglDriverKey -Force | Out-Null
76:         New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DLL -PropertyType String -Value "mesadrv.dll" -Force | Out-Null
77:         New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DriverVersion -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
78:         New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Flags -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
79:         New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Version -PropertyType DWord -Value 2 -Force | Out-Null
80: 
81:         $driverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Khronos\Vulkan\Drivers"
82:         New-Item -Path $driverKey -Force | Out-Null
83:         New-ItemProperty -Path $driverKey -Name $driverManifest.FullName -PropertyType DWord -Value 0 -Force | Out-Null
84: 
85:         "GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
86:         "Path=$mesaBin;$env:Path" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
87: 
88:     - name: Cache Cargo packages
89:       uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
90:       with:
91:         path: |
92:           ~/.cargo/registry/index/
93:           ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
94:           ~/.cargo/git/db/
95:         key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-cargo-registry-v1-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
96: 
97:     - name: Cache Gradle
98:       uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
99:       with:
100:         path: |
101:           ~/.gradle/caches
102:           ~/.gradle/wrapper
103:         key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
104:         restore-keys: |
105:           ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-
106: 
107:     - name: Trim Windows PATH before mise activation
108:       if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
109:       shell: pwsh
110:       run: |
111:         $workspaceDrive = Split-Path -Qualifier $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
112:         # Git Bash maps /tmp to the checkout drive; create it before mise hooks run.
113:         New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $workspaceDrive "tmp") | Out-Null
114: 
115:         $paths = @(
116:           if ($env:SCCACHE_PATH) { Split-Path -Parent $env:SCCACHE_PATH }
117:           if ($env:Path) { $env:Path -split ';' }
118:           "C:\Program Files\Git\bin",
119:           "C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin",
120:           "$env:SystemRoot\System32",
121:           "$env:SystemRoot",
122:           "$env:SystemRoot\System32\Wbem",
123:           "$env:SystemRoot\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0",
124:           "C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7",
125:           "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd"
126:         ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }
127: 
128:         "Path=$($paths -join ';')" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
129: 
130:         $gradleTemp = $env:RUNNER_TEMP -replace '\\', '/'
131:         $gradleOptions = "-Djava.io.tmpdir=$gradleTemp"
132:         if ($env:GRADLE_OPTS) {
133:           $gradleOptions = "$gradleOptions $env:GRADLE_OPTS"
134:         }
135:         "GRADLE_OPTS=$gradleOptions" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
136: 
137:     - name: Install mise toolchain
138:       uses: jdx/mise-action@1648a7812b9aeae629881980618f079932869151 # v4.0.1
139:       with:
140:         cache: true
141:         cache_key_prefix: mise-v2
142:         install: true
143: 
144:     - name: Wire sccache into CMake
145:       shell: bash
146:       run: |
147:         {
148:           echo "SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=true"
149:           echo "SCCACHE_GHA_VERSION=${MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE}"
150:           echo "SCCACHE_BASEDIRS=${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
151:           echo "CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
152:           echo "CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
153:         } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
154: 
155:     - name: Diagnose EGL setup
156:       if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
157:       shell: bash
158:       run: |
159:         mise exec -- bash -euo pipefail -c '
160:           env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
161:           command -v eglinfo
162:           EGL_LOG_LEVEL=debug LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose LD_DEBUG=libs eglinfo || true
163:         '
164: 
165:     - name: Diagnose Vulkan setup
166:       if: ${{ contains(env.MISE_ENV, 'vulkan') }}
167:       shell: bash
168:       run: mise run ci:vulkan-info
169: 
170:     # The mise action may restore Rust metadata without all rustup-managed
171:     # component files. Repair the toolchain only when rustup cannot add the
172:     # configured components or the component binaries still cannot run.
173:     - name: Ensure Rust components
174:       shell: bash
175:       run: |
176:         ensure_rust_components() {
177:           mise exec -- rustup component add rustfmt clippy &&
178:             mise exec -- cargo fmt --version &&
179:             mise exec -- cargo clippy --version
180:         }
181: 
182:         if ! ensure_rust_components; then
183:           mise install --force rust
184:           ensure_rust_components
185:         fi

(End of file - total 185 lines)

display.text:
name: Setup CI deps
description: Install sccache and mise toolchain

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Enable Git long paths
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: git config --global core.longpaths true

    - name: Cache MapLibre Native source
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          third_party/maplibre-native
          .git/modules/third_party/maplibre-native
        key: mln-src-v2-${{ hashFiles('.gitmodules', 'cmake/mln_version.cmake') }}

    - name: Install sccache
      uses: mozilla-actions/sccache-action@9e7fa8a12102821edf02ca5dbea1acd0f89a2696 # v0.0.10

    - name: Use Linux software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes \
          libegl-mesa0 \
          libgl1-mesa-dri \
          libvulkan1 \
          mesa-utils \
          mesa-utils-extra \
          mesa-vulkan-drivers
        {
          echo "EGL_PLATFORM=surfaceless"
          echo "LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=true"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Use Windows software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $mesaVersion = "26.0.8"
        $mesaArchive = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d.7z"
        $mesaRoot = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d"
        $mesaUrl = "https://github.com/pal1000/mesa-dist-win/releases/download/$mesaVersion/mesa3d-$mesaVersion-release-msvc.7z"

        Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $mesaUrl -OutFile $mesaArchive
        $expectedHash = "A438C26C2752726916E455F9AD121F8A7E3CFECF8626251ABF8E5B3E129D8497"
        $actualHash = (Get-FileHash $mesaArchive -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
        if ($actualHash -ne $expectedHash) {
          throw "Mesa archive hash mismatch: expected $expectedHash, got $actualHash"
        }
        7z x $mesaArchive "-o$mesaRoot" -y | Out-Null

        $mesaWglDriver = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "libgallium_wgl.dll" |
          Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "x64" } |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $mesaWglDriver) {
          throw "Missing Mesa WGL driver in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $driverManifest = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "lvp_icd.x86_64.json" |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $driverManifest) {
          throw "Missing lavapipe Vulkan ICD manifest in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $mesaBin = $mesaWglDriver.Directory.FullName
        $mesaWglSystemDriver = Join-Path $env:WINDIR "System32\mesadrv.dll"
        Copy-Item $mesaWglDriver.FullName $mesaWglSystemDriver -Force

        $openglDriverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\OpenGLDrivers\MSOGL"
        New-Item -Path $openglDriverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DLL -PropertyType String -Value "mesadrv.dll" -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DriverVersion -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Flags -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Version -PropertyType DWord -Value 2 -Force | Out-Null

        $driverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Khronos\Vulkan\Drivers"
        New-Item -Path $driverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $driverKey -Name $driverManifest.FullName -PropertyType DWord -Value 0 -Force | Out-Null

        "GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
        "Path=$mesaBin;$env:Path" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Cache Cargo packages
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.cargo/registry/index/
          ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
          ~/.cargo/git/db/
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-cargo-registry-v1-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}

    - name: Cache Gradle
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.gradle/caches
          ~/.gradle/wrapper
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-

    - name: Trim Windows PATH before mise activation
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $workspaceDrive = Split-Path -Qualifier $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
        # Git Bash maps /tmp to the checkout drive; create it before mise hooks run.
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $workspaceDrive "tmp") | Out-Null

        $paths = @(
          if ($env:SCCACHE_PATH) { Split-Path -Parent $env:SCCACHE_PATH }
          if ($env:Path) { $env:Path -split ';' }
          "C:\Program Files\Git\bin",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32",
          "$env:SystemRoot",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\Wbem",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0",
          "C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd"
        ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }

        "Path=$($paths -join ';')" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

        $gradleTemp = $env:RUNNER_TEMP -replace '\\', '/'
        $gradleOptions = "-Djava.io.tmpdir=$gradleTemp"
        if ($env:GRADLE_OPTS) {
          $gradleOptions = "$gradleOptions $env:GRADLE_OPTS"
        }
        "GRADLE_OPTS=$gradleOptions" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Install mise toolchain
      uses: jdx/mise-action@1648a7812b9aeae629881980618f079932869151 # v4.0.1
      with:
        cache: true
        cache_key_prefix: mise-v2
        install: true

    - name: Wire sccache into CMake
      shell: bash
      run: |
        {
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=true"
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_VERSION=${MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE}"
          echo "SCCACHE_BASEDIRS=${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
          echo "CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
          echo "CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Diagnose EGL setup
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        mise exec -- bash -euo pipefail -c '
          env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
          command -v eglinfo
          EGL_LOG_LEVEL=debug LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose LD_DEBUG=libs eglinfo || true
        '

    - name: Diagnose Vulkan setup
      if: ${{ contains(env.MISE_ENV, 'vulkan') }}
      shell: bash
      run: mise run ci:vulkan-info

    # The mise action may restore Rust metadata without all rustup-managed
    # component files. Repair the toolchain only when rustup cannot add the
    # configured components or the component binaries still cannot run.
    - name: Ensure Rust components
      shell: bash
      run: |
        ensure_rust_components() {
          mise exec -- rustup component add rustfmt clippy &&
            mise exec -- cargo fmt --version &&
            mise exec -- cargo clippy --version
        }

        if ! ensure_rust_components; then
          mise install --force rust
          ensure_rust_components
        fi

part.text:
Now I understand the CI structure. The "CI target support policy" is the variants matrix in ci.yml plus per-step `if:` conditions that encode which steps run on which platforms. That's the "hand-written policy" that drifts.

Goal: Make it declarative — generate the checked-in workflow from manifests, enforce sync. Contributors use `mise run ci:generate-workflow` and `mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check`.

Design considerations:
- Manifests: e.g. `.mise/ci/` or `ci/` directory with TOML manifests describing each variant (mise_env, runner, capabilities/skips). Or a single manifest file.
- Generator: a script (Python, since mise manages Python/uv and there's pyproject.toml + uv.lock; or Node; or Zig?). Let me check what's in pyproject.toml and scripts.

Let me check the Python setup and how tests are typically done. Also check hk.pkl and dprint.jsonc for lint integration so the generator gets formatted/checked.

Also "test the generator" — write tests for the generator. Python with pytest would be natural. Check pyproject.toml.

part.text:
No pytest installed. The project uses Python 3.14 with ruff + ty for linting. I could write the generator in Python with `unittest` from stdlib (no new dependency), or use Node. Python seems natural given uv-managed environment and ruff/ty linting.

Note: hk runs `ty check --error-on-warning .mise` — only on `.mise` directory. Interesting, so Python files under `.mise` get type-checked. That's a hint that Python scripts may already exist in .mise? Let me check. Actually .mise has only bin and tasks with shell scripts. The ty check on `.mise` suggests they plan for Python there, or it's defensive.

Design decision:
- Manifests: put them in `.mise/ci/` as TOML per variant, or a single `.mise/ci-variants.toml`? "Generate the checked-in workflow from manifests" — plural manifests. Options:
  1. One TOML per variant in `.mise/ci/variants/*.toml` — declarative, per-target policy.
  2. A single manifest file.

Given "CI target support policy", per-variant manifests declaring runner + which steps apply would be clean. But the step conditions are currently expressed as `if:` strings with patterns like `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`. To make it declarative, I can define steps with "capabilities" or "exclude" lists.

Simplest declarative approach that preserves behavior:

Manifest per variant (e.g. `.mise/ci/variants/linux-x64-vulkan.toml`):
```toml
mise_env = "linux-x64-vulkan"
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
```

And a shared manifest for steps? Or embed step policy in variant manifests: each variant lists which steps it runs. The current conditions:

Steps and their conditions:
1. `configure` — always
2. Build native library — if ios-* ; Test native library — if !ios-*
3. Build Java FFM binding — if != macos-arm64-egl && !ios-*
4. Build Java JNI binding — same
5. Test .NET binding — same
6. Test Kotlin/Native binding — != macos-arm64-egl && !ios-* && !windows-* && !linux-arm64-*
7. Test Rust binding — != macos-arm64-egl && !ios-*
8. Test Swift binding — != macos-arm64-egl && startsWith(macos-)
9. Test Zig binding — !ios-*
10. Build LWJGL example — contains(vulkan)
11. Test Rust example — != macos-arm64-egl && !ios-*
12. Build Swift example — == macos-arm64-metal
13. Build Zig example — != macos-arm64-egl && !ios-*
14. Run Zig readback example — !ios-*

Note "Test native library" vs "Build native library" on iOS is not really policy drift — it's a capability difference (can't run tests on iOS without a device).

Declarative model: define steps once (id, name, run command) with a default applicability, and per-variant manifests declare exclusions/capabilities. To preserve behavior exactly and keep it simple, I could model each variant with attributes:

```toml
# .mise/ci/variants/macos-arm64-metal.toml
mise_env = "macos-arm64-metal"
runner = "macos-latest"
```

And compute conditions from variant properties: platform (linux/macos/windows/ios), arch, backend. The conditions above are all expressible in terms of these attributes plus a couple of special cases (macos-arm64-egl exclusion, swift example only metal).

Option: derive conditions in the generator from variant attributes, with step definitions in a manifest like `.mise/ci/steps.toml`:

```toml
[[step]]
id = "java-ffm"
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
exclude = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
platforms = ["linux", "macos", "windows"]  # not ios
```

That is declarative and the generator emits `if:` conditions. But mapping platforms to `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')` conditions...

Alternatively, per-variant manifests could list the concrete steps they run, and the generator only creates the matrix + resolves which steps get which variants, emitting `if: contains(...)` conditions. Hmm, but that moves policy duplication into each manifest.

Let me think about what "declarative policy" means here. The policy is: which targets we support in CI and what runs on each. A clean design:

`.mise/ci/policy.toml` (or a directory of manifests):

```toml
[runners]
linux-arm64 = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
linux-x64 = "ubuntu-latest"
macos-arm64 = "macos-latest"
ios = "macos-latest"
windows-x64 = "windows-2022"

[[variants]]
env = "linux-arm64-egl"
...
```

And step applicability. I think the cleanest: keep one manifest directory `.mise/ci/` with:
- `variants/*.toml` — one file per CI variant, each declaring `mise_env`, `runner`, and `steps` overrides... 

Hmm, I'm overthinking. Let me consider the simplest design that satisfies the requirements:

1. Declarative manifests describing the CI target support policy (matrix + per-step applicability).
2. A generator script that renders `.github/workflows/ci.yml` from the manifests.
3. `mise run ci:generate-workflow` regenerates; `-- --check` fails if drifted.
4. CI enforces sync — add a step to the hygiene job running the check. But wait — hygiene job is part of the generated workflow. The generated workflow should include the drift-check step so CI enforces it. That's self-referential but fine.
5. Tests for the generator.

Simplest viable design:

- `ci/` directory at repo root? Or `.mise/ci/`? Given mise owns tasks and the ty check targets `.mise`, I'll put manifests and generator under `.mise/ci/`:
  - `.mise/ci/variants/*.toml` — 11 variant manifests (matching the 11 config.*.toml files). Actually could I derive variants from `.mise/config.*.toml` directly? The variant config files exist already — the manifest could just add runner + step applicability. Duplicating env names is the drift we want to avoid. Hmm, but "manifests" for CI policy is what the user asked for. I could have the variant manifests live alongside, and the generator cross-check that every `.mise/config.*.toml` has a CI manifest? Not all variants may be in CI though — currently all 11 are. The AGENTS.md lists 9 local envs, but CI has 11 (includes iOS ones).

Decision: manifests are per-variant TOML files in `.mise/ci/variants/`, one per CI target:

```toml
# ios-arm64-metal.toml
mise_env = "ios-arm64-metal"
runner = "macos-latest"
build_only = true   # cannot run tests without a device
exclude_steps = ["java-ffm", "java-jni", "dotnet", "kotlin-native", "rust", "swift", "zig", "rust-example", "swift-example", "zig-example"]
```

Hmm wait, but step conditions like `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')` for LWJGL — with per-variant step lists, the generator computes for each step the set of variants running it, then emits a condition. If a step runs on all variants → no `if`. Otherwise emit `if:` as a `contains(fromJSON(...))` check... That changes the workflow's shape but preserves behavior. "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy" — behavior = which steps run on which runners. Changing condition expressions from `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')` to an explicit list is arguably fine, but it makes the generated workflow less readable and changes diff massively.

Alternative that preserves the current condition style: define steps in a shared steps manifest with rule-based applicability, and variants with attributes. The generator renders conditions in the same style as today (`startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')` etc.), derived from declarative rules. This keeps the workflow diff minimal (only adding the hygiene drift-check step and a generated-file header).

Let me model:

Variant attributes (declarative in each variant manifest):
- `mise_env`
- `runner`
- `platform`: linux | macos | ios | windows (derivable from env prefix, actually! `linux-arm64-egl` → linux; `ios-simulator-arm64-metal` → ios; `windows-x64-vulkan` → windows). Platform is derivable from mise_env prefix. Backend also derivable (suffix). Arch too.

So actually the only non-derivable data per variant is the `runner`. And special-cases:
- `macos-arm64-egl` excluded from most binding steps (why? presumably ANGLE EGL on macOS lacks something — it's policy).
- Kotlin/Native excluded on windows and linux-arm64 (with comments linking to youtrack issues).
- Swift test on macos only (except egl).
- Swift example only on macos-arm64-metal.
- LWJGL example on vulkan variants.
- iOS: build-only.

Declarative step manifest with rules:

```toml
[[steps]]
id = "build-or-test-native"
# ios: build; others: test
```

I could encode each step as:

```toml
[[steps]]
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
[steps.when]
platforms = ["linux", "macos", "windows"]
exclude_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
```

Generator translates `when` into the existing-style expression:
- platforms excluding ios → `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- platforms == [macos] → `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-')`
- exclude_variants with single entry → `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl'`
- backend rule for lwjgl → `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')`
- variants == ["macos-arm64-metal"] → `matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal'`
- exclude_platforms windows & linux-arm64 for kotlin: `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-')` — linux-arm64 is platform+arch. Hmm.

This is getting complex. The risk: overengineering. The user says "Keep normal contributor workflow simple" and "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy."

Maybe simpler: variant manifests declare their step exclusions/inclusions directly in terms of step IDs, and the generator emits per-step conditions as JSON list membership. The checked-in workflow would look like:

```yaml
- name: Build Java FFM binding
  if: ${{ contains(fromJSON('["linux-arm64-egl", ...]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
```

Hmm, that's ugly but explicit. Or invert: keep step list per variant in the manifest:

```toml
# .mise/ci/variants/linux-x64-vulkan.toml
mise_env = "linux-x64-vulkan"
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
```

with the step policy in a single `steps.toml`. Steps have `skip_on` rules... 

OK here's another angle. What does "declarative" buy? The drift problem: the policy (supported targets) is defined in `.mise/config.*.toml` (11 variants), in AGENTS.md (9 listed), and in ci.yml matrix (11). The matrix should be generated from the variant manifests. Per-step conditions encode sub-policy.

I think a good, simple design:

**Manifest**: single `.mise/ci/targets.toml` — wait, user said "manifests" plural; per-variant files satisfy that naturally.

Let me go with:

```
.mise/ci/
  variants/
    linux-arm64-egl.toml
    ... (11 files)
  steps.toml        # shared step definitions with applicability rules
  generate_workflow.py
  test_generate_workflow.py
  workflow-template? 
```

Hmm, do I want a template for the static parts (hygiene, docs, required jobs, triggers)? The static parts are also "policy" but not target policy. Generating the whole workflow from a template embedded in Python or a Jinja file... Keep it simple: embed the workflow skeleton in the generator as a Python structure / string template, generate the whole file. Or keep a `ci.template.yml` with a placeholder for the matrix and steps.

Actually, maybe simplest robust approach: generator builds the entire ci.yml from:
1. A checked-in template `.mise/ci/workflow.template.yml` with placeholders like `# @generated:variants-matrix` — no, YAML templating with placeholders is fragile.

Better: generator constructs the full YAML via Python dict and dumps it. But PyYAML isn't in deps (dependencies = []). Python 3.14 stdlib has tomllib (for reading manifests) but no YAML. Writing YAML by hand with a small emitter for a fixed structure is doable but annoying — need `${{ }}` expressions, block scalars, comments (the checkout action has a pinned SHA comment, and kotlin has youtrack comments).

Options:
- Add PyYAML to dev deps (uv add). That changes uv.lock; fine. But generated YAML from PyYAML loses comments and style; the checked-in file would be fully machine-generated, which is the point. dprint pretty_yaml formats it afterwards. Comments like the youtrack links could be emitted... PyYAML can't do comments. ruamel.yaml can. Hmm.
- Hand-write an emitter with string templates. The workflow is mostly static; only the matrix entries and the `if:` conditions vary. A string-template generator is simple, fully controllable, preserves comments and formatting, and the "declarative" part lives in the manifests. I'll write the generator to assemble the YAML text directly.

Template approach: keep `workflow.yml` template text inside the Python generator? That buries workflow structure in Python. Better: `.mise/ci/workflow.template.yml` with explicit tokens like `{{VARIANTS_MATRIX}}` and `{{VARIANT_STEPS}}` that the generator replaces with rendered YAML blocks at known indentation. Simple string replacement, testable, and the template is reviewable YAML (actionlint can even lint it if tokens are quoted... no, tokens would break YAML parse; but actionlint only runs on .github/workflows/*.yml so template won't be linted — fine).

Hmm wait — should the whole workflow be generated, or just the variants job? The user: "Our CI target support policy is trapped in a hand-written workflow... Make it declarative, generate the checked-in workflow from manifests, and enforce that it stays in sync." → generate the checked-in workflow (ci.yml) from manifests. So ci.yml becomes fully generated. The template + manifests live in .mise/ci/.

Now the declarative step policy design. Let me define steps in `steps.toml` with applicability expressed declaratively, and the generator renders conditions. To preserve current expressions, I'll define a small rule model:

Each step:
- `name`, `run`
- applicability rules, all optional:
  - `platforms`: list of platforms where step runs (default all). Rendered:
    - if it's all except one prefix P: `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'P-')`
    - if single platform P: `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'P-')`
    - general: `(matrix.mise_env == '...' || ...)`? Not needed currently.
  - `exclude_variants`: list → `matrix.mise_env != 'x'` each, joined with &&
  - `only_variants`: single → `matrix.mise_env == 'x'`
  - `backends`: ['vulkan'] → `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')`
  - `exclude_targets`: for kotlin's linux-arm64 → `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-')` — that's platform+arch. Could model as `exclude_variants` listing linux-arm64-egl and linux-arm64-vulkan explicitly! That's even more declarative: `exclude_variants = ["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]`. Renders as 4 `!=` conditions. Changes the expression text but preserves behavior and is clearer policy. Hmm, but "preserve behavior" is satisfied; expression form may change. But it makes the checked-in file longer. Alternatively model `exclude` entries as prefixes: `exclude_prefixes = ["windows-", "linux-arm64-"]` → renders exactly the current expression with comments preserved. I like prefix-based rules since they mirror the current style and keep the workflow identical.

So the rule vocabulary:
- `skip_prefixes`: ["ios-"] → `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- `only_prefixes`: ["macos-"] → `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-')`
- `skip_variants`: ["macos-arm64-egl"] → `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl'`
- `only_variants`: ["macos-arm64-metal"] → `matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal'`
- `contains`: ["vulkan"] → `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')`

Conditions joined with ` && `. Order matters to reproduce current file: e.g. Java FFM is `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`. I'll define canonical ordering: skip_variants, only_variants, then prefixes, then contains. Let me check current orders:

- Java FFM: `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')` → skip_variants first, then skip_prefixes. ✓
- Kotlin: `!= 'macos-arm64-egl' && !ios- && !windows- && !linux-arm64-` ✓ same order.
- Swift test: `!= 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-')` → skip_variants then only_prefixes ✓.
- LWJGL: `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')` ✓.
- Swift example: `matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal'` ✓.

So canonical order: skip_variants, only_variants, skip_prefixes, only_prefixes, contains. That reproduces every current condition exactly.

The generator can also VALIDATE: every skip_variants/only_variants entry must be a known variant; skip_prefixes must match at least one variant; and it can cross-check manifests against `.mise/config.*.toml` — every variant config must have a CI manifest and vice versa (currently 1:1). That directly addresses "policy drift": adding `.mise/config.foo.toml` without CI manifest fails generation/check. Nice, that's enforcement beyond formatting.

Also runner mapping: manifest field `runner`.

The native build/test split: iOS builds only. Currently two steps:
```yaml
- name: Build native library
  if: startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')
  run: mise run build
- name: Test native library
  if: '!startsWith...'
  run: mise run test
```
These fit the step model fine as two steps.

Now the workflow template. ci.yml static parts: header comment (generated marker), name/on/permissions/concurrency, hygiene job (with added drift-check step), docs job, variants job shell, required job. The template contains everything with two substitution tokens: the matrix include entries and the variant steps. Actually also hygiene gets a new step `mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check`. Since hygiene is in the template, I just add it there.

Wait — but should the drift check live in the hygiene job? That enforces in CI that generated file is in sync. Yes.

Also: add `mise run ci:generate-workflow` task to mise.toml. Task with arg pass-through: `mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check`. In mise, task arguments after `--` are appended... Actually for file/toml tasks, extra args are passed as `$@`/appended. In mise TOML tasks, `run = "..."` gets args appended to the command line. `usage` for arg parsing is available but simple: define `run = "uv run .mise/ci/generate_workflow.py"` then args appended → `uv run script --check`. That works with `[tasks."ci:generate-workflow"]` and usage spec optional. Let me use mise's `usage` field for a proper --check flag? Simpler: just append. But mise appends extra args to the last run command for simple string runs. Yes: "Any additional arguments passed to the task will be appended to the run command". Good.

Tests: `test the generator` — write unit tests. No pytest in deps; use stdlib `unittest` (ruff/ty clean). Add a task? Maybe `ci:generate-workflow` tests run via... AGENTS.md testing guidance: tests should be high-value. I'll add unittest file and a mise task `ci:test-workflow-generator`? Or hook into existing test infra? The binding tests are heavy native tests; the generator is a CI-tooling script. I'll add a small task e.g. `[tasks."ci:test-generate-workflow"]`? Hmm keep it simple: tests run in hygiene? No—keep contributor workflow simple: `mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check` is the check. Tests for the generator itself: I can run them now to verify, and wire them into the `ci:lint`? That'd slow lint. Maybe add to hygiene job? Simpler: just run them locally via `uv run python -m unittest`. But then they rot. Let me add a mise task `ci:test-workflow` — hmm naming. I'll think: tasks named `ci:*` exist (`ci:lint`, `ci:vulkan-info`). I'll add `ci:generate-workflow` and put the unittest invocation as part of... Actually I can make the test part of the check task? No — check must be non-mutating drift check only, fast.

Decision: add task `ci:generate-workflow` (generate; `--check` for drift) and test file `.mise/ci/test_generate_workflow.py` runnable via `uv run python -m unittest discover .mise/ci` — and wire it into `ci:lint`? ci:lint runs hk fix + git diff. Adding generator tests to hygiene CI job: the hygiene job could run the unit tests too. That enforces in CI without burdening local lint. Hmm, but hygiene runs on the generated workflow... the drift check in hygiene covers sync; unit tests cover generator correctness. I'll add `mise run ci:test-workflow-generator`... name too long. Maybe just include unittest run as a second line in hygiene steps in the template: `run: mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check` plus `run: uv run python -m unittest ...`. Keep it minimal: one extra step "Check generated workflow is in sync" and one "Test workflow generator". Hmm, is that overkill? "test the generator" is an explicit requirement — tests must exist and run somewhere. Wiring into hygiene CI makes them enforced. I'll do it.

Where do manifests live? `.mise/ci/`. Note hk's `ty` step checks `uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise` — my Python files under .mise will be type-checked. Good, must be ty-clean. Also ruff via dprint/hk formats all *.py.

Manifest format — one TOML per variant:

```toml
# .mise/ci/variants/ios-arm64-metal.toml
# iOS device target: cross-compiled from macOS runners, no runtime tests.
mise_env = "ios-arm64-metal"
runner = "macos-latest"
```

And `steps.toml`:

```toml
# Declarative per-step applicability for the variants matrix job.
[[steps]]
name = "Build native library"
run = "mise run build"
only_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test native library"
run = "mise run test"
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Kotlin/Native binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build"
comments = [...]  # the youtrack comments
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-", "windows-", "linux-arm64-"]
...
```

Comments: the kotlin step has two comment lines. I can support `comments = ["...", "..."]` rendered above the step. Nice.

Configure step: `mise run configure` with no name/if — a step with no name? Current YAML: `- run: mise run configure` (no name key). Support optional name.

The checkout action pin: static in template.

Template with tokens. Let me design the template rendering:

...

{matrix}

Actually, hold on — is the template the right call vs generating everything from manifests? The user says "generate the checked-in workflow from manifests". Manifests (variants + steps) + template (static structure) → ci.yml. I think template + manifests is a clean interpretation; the *policy* (targets + what runs where) is fully manifest-driven. The template is structure, not policy.

Alternatively generate entire workflow in Python without template... I'll go with template; it's more maintainable and reviewable.

Now ordering of matrix entries: current file lists linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl, ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl. Also mixing: alphabetical across all → ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, linux-arm64-egl, ... Fine. I'll sort alphabetically and note it. Behavior unchanged.

Hmm wait, one nuance: "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy." Matrix ordering is not behavior. OK.

1. Each variant manifest's `mise_env` matches filename stem.
2. Manifest set == set of `.mise/config.*.toml` stems. (drift guard between local variant definitions and CI policy)
3. All variants referenced in step rules exist; all prefixes match ≥1 variant (catch stale policy like `windows-` when windows is dropped).
4. Render conditions deterministically.

Emitted `if:` uses `${{ ... }}` wrapper: current file mixes `if: ${{ ... }}`. I'll emit `${{ cond }}` always. Note in YAML, `if: ${{ ... }}` unquoted starting with `$` is fine. Current file has them unquoted. Keep same.

One YAML gotcha: conditions contain `:`? No. OK.

Drift check: `--check` regenerates to memory and diffs against `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; exit non-zero with message if different.

Also: after generation, is the file dprint-clean? ci:lint runs `hk fix --all` which runs dprint on the yml and then `git diff --exit-code`. If my generator emits formatting that dprint would change, ci:lint fails. So I must run dprint on the generated file (or emit dprint-clean YAML). g-plane/pretty_yaml formats workflow yml. I can verify by running `hk fix`/dprint locally after generating. Let me plan to run `mise exec -- dprint fmt .github/workflows/ci.yml` after generation and check it's stable; adjust emitter until stable. Even better: the generator could invoke dprint itself... no, keep it simple, match formatting manually. pretty_yaml: 2-space indents, keeps comments. My emitter must produce exact indentation. I'll iterate.

Also actionlint will lint the workflow — keep it valid.

```

And generator unit tests step:
```yaml

```

Actually, cleanest: the generator module includes tests, and hygiene job runs `uv run --no-sync python -m unittest ...`? uv run triggers sync; fine in CI since postinstall ran uv sync. I'll define a mise task `"ci:test-workflow"` hmm... Let me just name it `ci:generate-workflow:test`? Mise supports colons. I'll keep it flat: `ci:workflow-generator-test`? Overthinking. Decide:

In hygiene, steps run through `mise run ci:lint` so mise is on PATH via mise-action. `uv` binary is mise-installed; in a `run` step without mise activation... mise-action v4 activates mise? I'll wire the unittest into the `ci:lint` task? No — ci:lint is run by contributors via fix hooks... Actually contributors rarely run ci:lint manually. Adding generator tests to ci:lint means hygiene needs no new step and tests are enforced. But ci:lint is "lint"; adding tests muddies it. I'll add a dedicated hygiene step in the template:

```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE description="Init only MapLibre Native submodules required for maplibre-native-ffi builds"
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="${MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)}"
mln_path="third_party/maplibre-native"

cd "$repo_root"

# Paths relative to the maplibre-native submodule (not the FFI repo root).
# Nested test/mason/glfw trees inside these repos are not fetched (submodule.recurse=false).
vendor_paths=(
  vendor/earcut.hpp # polygon triangulation
  vendor/polylabel # polygon label placement
  vendor/protozero # MVT / glyph PBF
  vendor/vector-tile # MVT decode headers
  vendor/wagyu # geometry clipping
  vendor/unique_resource # OpenGL headless RAII
  vendor/boost # header-only utilities
  vendor/rapidjson # style JSON (not thirdparty/gtest)
  vendor/eternal # interned strings
  vendor/expected-lite # expected<> types
  vendor/filesystem # ghc::filesystem shim
  vendor/unordered_dense # hash maps
  vendor/supercluster # clustering
  vendor/kdbush.hpp # spatial index
  vendor/PMTiles # PMTiles headers (MLN_WITH_PMTILES)
  vendor/maplibre-tile-spec # mlt-cpp (MLT JSON/FASTPFOR/TESTS off in FFI)
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/cheap-ruler-cpp
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/geojson-vt-cpp
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/geojson.hpp
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/geometry.hpp
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/shelf-pack-cpp
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/variant
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/jni.hpp # configure-time base dep
  vendor/maplibre-native-base/deps/pixelmatch-cpp # configure-time base dep
  vendor/freetype # text shaping (MLN_TEXT_SHAPING_HARFBUZZ)
  vendor/harfbuzz
  vendor/Vulkan-Headers # Vulkan headers + Zig bindgen include path
  vendor/VulkanMemoryAllocator
  vendor/glslang # SPIR-V toolchain (MLN_WITH_VULKAN)
)

# Skipped by design (full MapLibre tree only): googletest, benchmark, cpp-httplib, args,
# zip-archive, webgpu-cpp, wgpu-native, docs/doxygen, platform/windows/vendor/vcpkg, and
# nested .mason / test fixture / glfw submodules under the paths above.

git -c submodule.recurse=false submodule sync "$mln_path"
git -c submodule.recurse=false submodule update --init --depth 1 "$mln_path"

git -C "$mln_path" -c submodule.recurse=false submodule sync "${vendor_paths[@]}"
git -C "$mln_path" -c submodule.recurse=false submodule update --init --depth 1 "${vendor_paths[@]}"
#!/usr/bin/env python
import glob
import os
import subprocess
import sys


def main() -> int:
    build_dir = os.environ["MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"]
    if os.environ.get("CI") != "true":
        return subprocess.call(["mise", "run", "//:build"])

    patterns = [
        os.path.join(build_dir, "libmaplibre-native-c.*"),
        os.path.join(build_dir, "maplibre-native-c.*"),
        os.path.join("build", "libmaplibre-native-c.*"),
        os.path.join("build", "maplibre-native-c.*"),
    ]
    if any(glob.glob(pattern) for pattern in patterns):
        return 0

    print(
        f"missing {build_dir}/[lib]maplibre-native-c.*; native artifact was not downloaded",
        file=sys.stderr,
    )
    return 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

part.text:
Good — ci.yml is the only place with the matrix. Now let me plan the design and possibly ask the user about architecture. The user explicitly said "You may use ask_user for consequential architecture or scope questions." Key decisions:

1. Manifest location/format: `.mise/ci/variants/*.toml` + `.mise/ci/steps.toml` + template. 
2. Generator language: Python (project already has Python file tasks, uv-managed, ruff/ty lint it).

I think these are reasonably inferable; the user said "may" ask. The design is fairly well-constrained. One consequential question could be whether the whole workflow is generated from a template vs. only the matrix. But I'll decide: generate the whole ci.yml from a checked-in template + manifests. That seems within scope and I shouldn't pester.

Let me set up a todo list and start implementing.

Plan:
1. Create `.mise/ci/variants/*.toml` — 11 manifests (mise_env, runner, optional comment).
2. Create `.mise/ci/steps.toml` — declarative step policy for the variants job.
3. Create `.mise/ci/workflow.template.yml` — static workflow structure with tokens.
4. Create `.mise/ci/generate_workflow.py` — generator with --check mode, validations.
5. Create `.mise/ci/test_generate_workflow.py` — unittest tests.
6. Add `ci:generate-workflow` task in mise.toml; hygiene job runs check + tests.
7. Generate ci.yml; verify byte-diff vs old (except intended changes: header comment, hygiene steps, matrix ordering).
8. Run dprint/actionlint/ruff/ty; run generator tests; run `--check`.
9. Update AGENTS.md (workflow section mentions CI target policy) + docs overview if needed.
10. Commit.

Important detail: reproduce current conditions exactly. Let me enumerate steps with current conditions in order:

1. `- run: mise run configure` (no name, no if)
2. name "Build native library", if startsWith ios-, run build
3. name "Test native library", if !startsWith ios-, run test
4. "Build Java FFM binding", if != egl && !ios-, run //bindings/java-ffm:build
5. "Build Java JNI binding", same cond
6. "Test .NET binding", same cond
7. "Test Kotlin/Native binding", comments (2 lines), if != egl && !ios- && !windows- && !linux-arm64-
8. "Test Rust binding", if != egl && !ios-
9. "Test Swift binding", if != egl && startsWith macos-
10. "Test Zig binding", if !ios-
11. "Build LWJGL example", if contains vulkan
12. "Test Rust example", if != egl && !ios-
13. "Build Swift example", if == macos-arm64-metal
14. "Build Zig example", if != egl && !ios-
15. "Run Zig readback example", if !ios-

Condition canonical ordering to reproduce: skip_variants (!=), only_variants (==), skip_prefixes (!startsWith), only_prefixes (startsWith), contains. Check #9: `!= egl && startsWith macos-` → skip_variants then only_prefixes ✓.

Manifest schema for steps.toml:

```toml
[[steps]]
run = "mise run configure"

[[steps]]
name = "Build native library"
run = "mise run build"
only_prefixes = ["ios-"]
...
[[steps]]
name = "Test Kotlin/Native binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build"
comments = [
  "linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871",
  "windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446",
]
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-", "windows-", "linux-arm64-"]
```

Wait — skip_prefixes renders as `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-')`. But "linux-arm64-" is not a platform prefix, it's platform-arch; still works with startsWith. Good.

Validation: each skip_prefix/only_prefix must match at least one variant (current ones all do). skip_variants/only_variants must be existing variants. Also maybe warn if a step ends up running on zero variants → error.

Variant manifests:

```toml
# .mise/ci/variants/linux-x64-vulkan.toml
mise_env = "linux-x64-vulkan"
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
```

Should I include a doc comment capability? TOML comments in the manifest files themselves serve documentation. Also could validate runner naming conventions? Skip.

Cross-check with .mise/config.*.toml: the generator validates sets equal. 

Template: I'll write `.mise/ci/workflow.template.yml` containing the full workflow with two marker lines:

```
          # @variants-matrix
```
and
```
      # @variant-steps
```

Hmm, markers as comments keep template parseable YAML-ish. The generator replaces the marker line with rendered block (marker line carries the indentation baseline; rendered lines are emitted at their full indentation, replacing the marker entirely).

Wait, but the hygiene job's new steps (drift check, generator tests) live in the template — but the tests step references the test file. Fine.

Actually, should the drift check be in the template or injected by generator? Template — it's static. OK.

Header: generated marker comment at top:
```yaml
# Generated by `mise run ci:generate-workflow` from the manifests in .mise/ci/.
# Edit those manifests and regenerate; do not edit this file directly.
```
Put this IN the template so template == generated modulo tokens. Good.

Runner per variant — sorted alphabetically by mise_env in matrix. Current order is grouped by platform. Alphabetical: ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl, macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan, windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl. Behavior-neutral.

Now the generator implementation. Python 3.14, stdlib only (tomllib, argparse, pathlib, difflib, re). Must pass ruff + ty (`ty check --error-on-warning .mise`).

Structure:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
"""Render .github/workflows/ci.yml from the CI policy manifests in .mise/ci/."""
```

Functions:
- load_variants(dir) -> list[Variant] (dataclass with mise_env, runner), sorted by mise_env. Validate filename stem == mise_env; validate runner non-empty str; unknown keys error.
- load_steps(path) -> list[Step]. Validate keys; run required; rule keys known.
- validate_policy(variants, steps, mise_dir): variant set == config.*.toml stems; prefixes match ≥1 variant; variant refs exist; each step applies to ≥1 variant; skip+only combos don't zero out.
- condition_for(step) -> str | None.
- render_matrix(variants) -> str lines:
  ```
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
  ```
  Matrix entries at 10 spaces indent for `- mise_env` (current file: line 45 `          - mise_env:` = 10 spaces). runner continuation 12 spaces.
- render_steps(steps) -> str: each step block at 6-space indent list under `steps:`:
  ```
      - run: mise run configure

      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
  ```
  Blank line between configure group and the rest matches current file (line 71-72: configure then blank line? Current: line 71 `- run: mise run configure`, line 72 `- name: Build native library` — no blank line actually. Let me re-check: lines 67-77:

```
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
```

So blank line after setup-ci-deps, then configure, then named steps contiguous. The generator renders steps after the setup steps in template; template has the checkout/setup and a token on following line. I'll place token `      # @variant-steps` after a blank line following setup-ci-deps. Rendered replaces token.

Kotlin comments: rendered before the step:
```
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://...
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://...
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
```

- apply_template(template, matrix_block, steps_block) -> str: replace marker lines.
- main: argparse `--check`. Write or compare; on drift print diff hint and return 1.

Paths: resolve repo root relative to the script file (script is at .mise/ci/generate_workflow.py → parents[2]). But also support MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT env. Use `Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]`.

Tests: unittest with tempfile dirs constructing fake manifests, testing:
- condition rendering for each rule type & ordering
- validation errors (unknown variant in skip_variants; prefix matching nothing; missing variant manifest for a config; step applying to no variants)
- end-to-end: build a small fake tree (variants/, steps.toml, template with markers, .mise config stubs?) Hmm, the config cross-check needs .mise/config.*.toml. Make functions take paths so tests can point at tmp dirs. Also a test that the real repo manifests generate the checked-in ci.yml? That's what --check does; a unit test asserting `render(repo) == ci.yml` duplicates --check but is a nice guard runnable via unittest. Mise selects the native build profile that matches your host when one is
available. Set `MISE_ENV=<variant>` before `mise run ...`, or pass
`mise -E <variant> run ...`, to build, test, or run examples for another
platform and render backend.

## Common Commands

```bash
# Build and test the C API (via Zig bindings)
mise run test

# Build only
mise run build

# Run linters and formatters
mise run fix
`mise run check`, and `mise run fix`. [`dprint`](https://dprint.dev/) owns
repository-wide formatting defaults.

[Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/)
build the documentation site. Generated API reference HTML is installed into
`docs/public/reference/` before each docs build.

## Tests And Examples

Every feature needs automated CI coverage when practical. The root
`mise run test` command builds the native library, runs the direct Zig C API
suite, and runs the public Zig binding suite for supported host variants.

Use examples for demos and behavior that needs manual validation, such as visual
output, interactive input, or host graphics integration.

Keep examples small. This repository includes low-level language bindings and
focused integration examples. Full application SDKs live outside this
repository.
