input.content:
# Declarative CI step policy for the variants matrix job.
#
# Each [[steps]] entry becomes one step in .github/workflows/ci.yml. `run` is
# required; `name` and `comments` are optional. Applicability rules select the
# variants a step runs on (a variant must survive every rule):
#
#   skip_variants  — exact mise_env values to exclude
#   only_variants  — exact mise_env values to include
#   skip_prefixes  — exclude variants whose mise_env starts with any prefix
#   only_prefixes  — include only variants whose mise_env starts with a prefix
#   contains       — include only variants whose mise_env contains the substring
#
# Regenerate the workflow with `mise run ci:generate-workflow`.

[[steps]]
run = "mise run configure"

# CI has no iOS device, so ios- variants build the native library instead of
# running the test suites.
[[steps]]
name = "Build native library"
run = "mise run build"
only_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test native library"
run = "mise run test"
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Java JNI binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/java-jni:build"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test .NET binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/dotnet:test"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Kotlin/Native binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build"
comments = [
  "linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871",
  "windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446",
]
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-", "windows-", "linux-arm64-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Rust binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/rust:ci"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Swift binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/swift:test"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
only_prefixes = ["macos-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Zig binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/zig:ci"
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build LWJGL example"
run = "mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build"
contains = ["vulkan"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Rust example"
run = "mise run //examples/rust-map:ci"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Swift example"
run = "mise run //examples/swift-map:build"
only_variants = ["macos-arm64-metal"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Zig example"
run = "mise run //examples/zig-map:build"
skip_variants = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

[[steps]]
name = "Run Zig readback example"
run = "mise run //examples/zig-readback:run"
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]

input.content:
# Generated by `mise run ci:generate-workflow` from the CI policy manifests in
# .mise/ci/. Edit those manifests and regenerate; do not edit the output by hand.
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint
      - run: mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check
      - run: mise exec -- python -m unittest discover -s .mise/ci -t .mise/ci -v

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          # @generated:variant-matrix
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      # @generated:variant-steps

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

part.text: Now the workflow template:

Now the generator. Careful with:
- stdlib only (tomllib, argparse, dataclasses, pathlib, sys, difflib)
- ty --error-on-warning clean: full annotations
- ruff format clean (88 cols)

Structure:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
"""Generate .github/workflows/ci.yml from the CI policy manifests in .mise/ci/."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import difflib
import sys
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
CI_DIR = REPO_ROOT / ".mise" / "ci"
WORKFLOW_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"

MATRIX_MARKER = "# @generated:variant-matrix"
STEPS_MARKER = "# @generated:variant-steps"

VARIANT_KEYS = {"mise_env", "runner"}
STEP_KEYS = {...}

class PolicyError(Exception): ...

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Variant:
    mise_env: str
    runner: str

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Step:
    run: str
    name: str | None = None
    comments: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    skip_variants: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    only_variants: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    skip_prefixes: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    only_prefixes: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    contains: tuple[str, ...] = ()
```

Parsing helpers: require_str, require_str_list.

applies_to(step, mise_env) -> bool.

render_condition(step) -> str | None.

validate(variants, steps, config_envs).

render_matrix, render_steps, render_workflow(ci_dir, workflow_path for check?) — make render_workflow(ci_dir: Path, mise_dir: Path) -> str.

main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int.

Careful with tomllib types: dict[str, Any]. For ty, I'll validate types explicitly with isinstance and raise PolicyError. ty should handle narrowings.

Writing:

def load_variants(ci_dir):
    variants_dir = ci_dir / "variants"
    paths = sorted(variants_dir.glob("*.toml"))
    if not paths: raise PolicyError(f"no variant manifests found in {variants_dir}")
    variants = []
    for path in paths:
        data = tomllib.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        unknown = set(data) - VARIANT_KEYS
        if unknown: raise PolicyError(...)
        mise_env = data.get("mise_env"); runner = data.get("runner")
        if not isinstance(mise_env, str) or not mise_env: raise ...
        if not isinstance(runner, str) or not runner: raise ...
        if path.stem != mise_env: raise PolicyError(...)
        variants.append(Variant(mise_env=mise_env, runner=runner))
    variants.sort(key=lambda v: v.mise_env)
    return variants

render_workflow(ci_dir, mise_dir):
    variants = load_variants(ci_dir)
    steps = load_steps(ci_dir)
    config_envs = {p.stem.removeprefix("config.") for p in mise_dir.glob("config.*.toml")}
    validate(variants, steps, config_envs)
    template = (ci_dir / "workflow.template.yml").read_text()
    out_lines = []
    for line in template.splitlines():
        stripped = line.strip()
        if stripped == MATRIX_MARKER:
            out_lines.extend(render_matrix(variants))
        elif stripped == STEPS_MARKER:
            out_lines.extend(render_steps(steps))
        else:
            out_lines.append(line)
    return "\n".join(out_lines) + "\n"

def applies_to(step, env):
    if env in step.skip_variants: return False
    if step.only_variants and env not in step.only_variants: return False
    if any(env.startswith(p) for p in step.skip_prefixes): return False
    if step.only_prefixes and not any(env.startswith(p) for p in step.only_prefixes): return False
    return all(c in env for c in step.contains)

#!/usr/bin/env python
"""Tests for the CI workflow generator in generate_workflow.py."""

import tempfile
import unittest
from pathlib import Path

import generate_workflow as gw

TEMPLATE = """header
        include:
          # @generated:variant-matrix
    steps:
      # @generated:variant-steps
footer
"""

STEPS_TOML = """[[steps]]
run = "mise run configure"

[[steps]]
name = "Test native library"
run = "mise run test"
skip_prefixes = ["ios-"]
"""


def make_tree(
    root: Path,
    variant_envs: list[str],
    steps_toml: str = STEPS_TOML,
    config_envs: list[str] | None = None,
) -> tuple[Path, Path]:
    ci_dir = root / ".mise" / "ci"
    mise_dir = root / ".mise"
    (ci_dir / "variants").mkdir(parents=True)
    for env in variant_envs:
        (ci_dir / "variants" / f"{env}.toml").write_text(
            f'mise_env = "{env}"\nrunner = "ubuntu-latest"\n', encoding="utf-8"
        )
    for env in config_envs if config_envs is not None else variant_envs:
        (mise_dir / f"config.{env}.toml").write_text("", encoding="utf-8")
    (ci_dir / "steps.toml").write_text(steps_toml, encoding="utf-8")
    (ci_dir / "workflow.template.yml").write_text(TEMPLATE, encoding="utf-8")
    return ci_dir, mise_dir


class RenderConditionTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_no_rules_has_no_condition(self) -> None:
        self.assertIsNone(gw.render_condition(gw.Step(run="mise run configure")))

    def test_single_rules(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(
            gw.render_condition(gw.Step(run="x", skip_variants=("a-b-c",))),
            "matrix.mise_env != 'a-b-c'",
        )
        self.assertEqual(
            gw.render_condition(gw.Step(run="x", only_variants=("a-b-c",))),
            "matrix.mise_env == 'a-b-c'",
        )
        self.assertEqual(
            gw.render_condition(gw.Step(run="x", skip_prefixes=("ios-",))),
            "!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')",
        )
        self.assertEqual(
            gw.render_condition(gw.Step(run="x", only_prefixes=("macos-",))),
            "startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-')",
        )
        self.assertEqual(
            gw.render_condition(gw.Step(run="x", contains=("vulkan",))),
            "contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')",
        )

    def test_canonical_rule_ordering(self) -> None:
        step = gw.Step(
            run="x",
            skip_variants=("macos-arm64-egl",),
            skip_prefixes=("ios-", "windows-"),
        )
        self.assertEqual(
            gw.render_condition(step),
            "matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && "
            "!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && "
            "!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-')",
        )


class AppliesToTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_rules_combine(self) -> None:
        step = gw.Step(
            run="x", skip_variants=("macos-arm64-egl",), only_prefixes=("macos-",)
        )
        self.assertTrue(gw.applies_to(step, "macos-arm64-metal"))
        self.assertFalse(gw.applies_to(step, "macos-arm64-egl"))
        self.assertFalse(gw.applies_to(step, "linux-x64-vulkan"))


class LoadTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_variants_are_sorted(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, _ = make_tree(Path(tmp), ["b-x-egl", "a-x-egl"])
            self.assertEqual(
                [v.mise_env for v in gw.load_variants(ci_dir)],
                ["a-x-egl", "b-x-egl"],
            )

    def test_variant_file_name_must_match_mise_env(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, _ = make_tree(Path(tmp), ["a-x-egl"])
            (ci_dir / "variants" / "a-x-egl.toml").write_text(
                'mise_env = "other"\nrunner = "ubuntu-latest"\n', encoding="utf-8"
            )
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                gw.load_variants(ci_dir)

    def test_unknown_keys_are_rejected(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, _ = make_tree(Path(tmp), ["a-x-egl"])
            (ci_dir / "variants" / "a-x-egl.toml").write_text(
                'mise_env = "a-x-egl"\nrunner = "r"\nos = "x"\n', encoding="utf-8"
            )
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                gw.load_variants(ci_dir)
            (ci_dir / "steps.toml").write_text(
                '[[steps]]\nrun = "x"\nplatform = "linux"\n', encoding="utf-8"
            )
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                gw.load_steps(ci_dir)


class ValidateTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def validate(self, tmp: str, steps_toml: str, config_envs: list[str]) -> None:
        ci_dir, mise_dir = make_tree(
            Path(tmp), ["linux-x64-vulkan"], steps_toml, config_envs
        )
        variants = gw.load_variants(ci_dir)
        steps = gw.load_steps(ci_dir)
        envs = {p.stem.removeprefix("config.") for p in mise_dir.glob("config.*")}
        gw.validate(variants, steps, envs)

    def test_unknown_variant_reference(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                self.validate(
                    tmp,
                    '[[steps]]\nrun = "x"\nskip_variants = ["nope"]\n',
                    ["linux-x64-vulkan"],
                )

    def test_prefix_must_match_a_variant(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                self.validate(
                    tmp,
                    '[[steps]]\nrun = "x"\nskip_prefixes = ["ios-"]\n',
                    ["linux-x64-vulkan"],
                )

    def test_step_must_apply_to_some_variant(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                self.validate(
                    tmp,
                    '[[steps]]\nrun = "x"\nskip_variants = ["linux-x64-vulkan"]\n',
                    ["linux-x64-vulkan"],
                )

    def test_variants_must_match_mise_configs(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                self.validate(tmp, STEPS_TOML, ["linux-x64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl"])
            with self.assertRaises(gw.PolicyError):
                self.validate(tmp, STEPS_TOML, [])


class RenderWorkflowTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_markers_are_replaced(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, mise_dir = make_tree(Path(tmp), ["linux-x64-vulkan"])
            rendered = gw.render_workflow(ci_dir, mise_dir)
        self.assertEqual(
            rendered,
            """header
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test
footer
""",
        )

    def test_comments_render_above_their_step(self) -> None:
        steps_toml = (
            '[[steps]]\nname = "X"\nrun = "x"\n'
            'comments = ["why", "where"]\ncontains = ["vulkan"]\n'
        )
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, mise_dir = make_tree(
                Path(tmp), ["linux-x64-vulkan"], steps_toml
            )
            rendered = gw.render_workflow(ci_dir, mise_dir)
        self.assertIn(
            "      # why\n"
            "      # where\n"
            "      - name: X\n"
            "        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}\n"
            "        run: x\n",
            rendered,
        )

    def test_repository_manifests_render(self) -> None:
        rendered = gw.render_workflow(gw.CI_DIR, gw.MISE_DIR)
        for marker in (gw.MATRIX_MARKER, gw.STEPS_MARKER):
            self.assertNotIn(marker, rendered)


class MainTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_write_then_check(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, mise_dir = make_tree(Path(tmp), ["linux-x64-vulkan"])
            workflow = Path(tmp) / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"
            workflow.parent.mkdir(parents=True)
            with (
                unittest.mock.patch.object(gw, "CI_DIR", ci_dir),
                unittest.mock.patch.object(gw, "MISE_DIR", mise_dir),
                unittest.mock.patch.object(gw, "WORKFLOW_PATH", workflow),
                unittest.mock.patch.object(gw, "REPO_ROOT", Path(tmp)),
            ):
                self.assertEqual(gw.main(["--check"]), 1)  # not generated yet
                self.assertEqual(gw.main([]), 0)
                self.assertEqual(gw.main(["--check"]), 0)
                workflow.write_text("drifted\n", encoding="utf-8")
                self.assertEqual(gw.main(["--check"]), 1)

    def test_invalid_policy_fails(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            ci_dir, mise_dir = make_tree(Path(tmp), ["linux-x64-vulkan"])
            (ci_dir / "steps.toml").write_text("[[steps]]\n", encoding="utf-8")
            with (
                unittest.mock.patch.object(gw, "CI_DIR", ci_dir),
                unittest.mock.patch.object(gw, "MISE_DIR", mise_dir),
            ):
                self.assertEqual(gw.main([]), 2)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

part.text: The environment: `mise trust --all` triggered a full reinstall into /opt/mise-data which is read-only (permission denied). The tools were previously installed somewhere. MISE_DATA_DIR is /opt/mise-data? The tools are presumably already installed but trust got reset. Let me check: maybe tools exist but installs dir listing... The error says failed create_dir_all in /opt/mise-data/installs — permission denied. So the environment preinstalled mise tools in /opt/mise-data as root, and we're running as `agent`. Previously `mise run` worked? I never ran it successfully. The trust issue: "Config files ... are not trusted" — because I edited mise.toml (config trust is per-file checksum). `mise trust --all` re-trusted, but then it tried to install missing tools. Maybe tools are installed but under a different data dir used earlier? Let me check what's in /opt/mise-data and whether e.g. python shim exists.
The trust reset triggered a tool reinstall into a read-only directory. Let me inspect the environment:

part.text:
Only python is preinstalled via mise. Other tools (dprint, hk, actionlint, etc.) are not installed and can't be installed (read-only /opt/mise-data, probably no network either). So I can't run dprint/hk/actionlint locally. I have python 3.14 (both system /usr/local/bin/python3 and .venv). So:

- Generator: run with python directly.
- Unit tests: run with python -m unittest.
- dprint formatting: can't verify with dprint; I must be careful to emit pretty_yaml-compatible formatting manually. The existing ci.yml passed dprint before, and my template copies its formatting; generated sections replicate the original formatting. Should be fine.
- ruff: /app/.venv/bin/ruff exists! And ty. I can run those directly on .mise/ci.
- actionlint: not available; I'll validate YAML with python (yaml module? PyYAML may not be installed). Check .venv for yaml. Python 3.14 stdlib has no yaml. I can at least sanity-check by eye.

Avoid running `mise run` again since it wants to install missing tools (mise run triggers install of missing tools? It failed on postinstall hooks). Actually the failure was because trust --all triggered reinstall of ALL tools. Why would it reinstall? Perhaps because trust --all marked config trusted, then `mise run` resolved env and saw tools missing (only python installed) and tried to install all → permission denied. So `mise run` won't work in this environment at all for tasks requiring env? Wait — how did the environment intend `mise run ci:generate-workflow` to work then? Maybe with MISE_DATA_DIR read-only but tools... only python installed. Hmm, maybe there's network and installs work elsewhere? /opt/mise-data is root-owned. Perhaps the intended flow: `mise install` was run as root at image build with only python? No — mise.lock exists, and pixi env exists (/app/.pixi? let me check). The AGENTS.md says mise install sets everything up. In this sandbox, likely only partial.

For my task, I don't strictly need mise to run — the generator only needs python3.14 (stdlib tomllib). The task definition `run = "python .mise/ci/generate_workflow.py"` requires `python` on PATH; in CI mise provides it. Locally I can verify with /app/.venv/bin/python or python3.

Let me just run the generator with python3 directly. Also verify `--check` behavior. Note: REPO_ROOT is derived from __file__, so it works regardless of cwd.

state.title: cd /app && python3 .mise/ci/generate_workflow.py && git diff .github/workflows/ci.yml

part.text:
Excellent — very close. The diff is minimal:
1. Header comment (intended)
2. Hygiene steps (intended)
3. Matrix reordering alphabetical (intended, behavior-neutral)
4. Two blank lines removed: after "Test native library" step and after "Test Zig binding" step — the original had blank lines separating logical groups (native, bindings, examples). I should preserve grouping blank lines for readability. Options: add `group` concept or a `blank_before = true` option in steps.toml. Simplest declarative approach: support `comments` already; add blank line before a step when it has comments? Kotlin step has comments but no blank line before it in the original... Original: comments at lines 88-89 directly follow the dotnet step run line (line 87 → 88 comment). So no blank before kotlin comments.

The original blank lines: line 78 blank (before "Build Java FFM binding" — separates native group from bindings group), line 102 blank (before "Build LWJGL example" — separates bindings from examples). These map to group boundaries. I could add `new_group = true` on those steps... Or infer from comments? No. Add a boolean `section_break = true`? Hmm, that's presentational, not policy. But preserving readability of the checked-in file matters. Add a `blank_before = true` key to steps.toml for "Build Java FFM binding" and "Build LWJGL example". Simple.

Hmm, alternatively organize steps.toml with groups... Keep it simple: `blank_before` bool. But wait — dprint pretty_yaml might remove or keep blank lines between sequence items; original file kept them, so they're stable.

Implement: Step.blank_before: bool = False; render "\n" line before comments/step when true (but not for first step... configure is first; blanks only used mid-list, fine). Blank line = empty string in lines list.

Let me update generator, steps.toml, and regenerate. Also note template has blank line after setup-ci-deps and marker line, then blank line before `required:` — check rendered output around those: template:

```
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      # @generated:variant-steps

  required:
```
Rendered: steps block followed by blank line then `  required:` — matches original (blank line at 118 before required). Good, diff shows no change there.

state.title: cd /app && .venv/bin/ty check --error-on-warning .mise 2>&1 | head -60

part.text:
Three test bugs:
1. `test_markers_are_replaced`: STEPS_TOML uses skip_prefixes=["ios-"] but the tree only has linux-x64-vulkan → validation error. Fix make_tree default envs to include an ios variant, or change expected rendering. I'll add "ios-arm64-metal" to the tree in those tests and update expected output. Simpler: in make_tree default variant_envs include ios-arm64-metal. For test_markers_are_replaced expected output then includes two matrix entries. Let me set variants ["linux-x64-vulkan", "ios-arm64-metal"] there and update expected string (sorted: ios-arm64-metal first).

2. `test_variants_must_match_mise_configs`: calls self.validate twice in same tmp → make_tree mkdir fails with exist_ok. Add exist_ok=True.

3. `test_write_then_check`: main(["--check"]) returned 2 because... workflow path missing → check should return 1, but it returned 2 → PolicyError raised. Why? Because STEPS_TOML has ios- prefix but tree lacks ios variant → PolicyError → return 2. Same root cause as #1. Fix by including ios variant in the tree (update make_tree default or pass envs).

Fix: make_tree default variant list — I'll change the three failing call sites to pass ["ios-arm64-metal", "linux-x64-vulkan"]. Actually simpler: change STEPS_TOML used in tests to not require ios... but testing skip_prefixes rendering with ios is nice. I'll add the ios variant where STEPS_TOML is used:
- test_markers_are_replaced: variants ["ios-arm64-metal", "linux-x64-vulkan"]; expected output includes both entries sorted: ios first.
- ValidateTest.test_variants_must_match_mise_configs uses STEPS_TOML → its validate() helper builds tree with ["linux-x64-vulkan"] only → prefix error masks the intended config-mismatch error? The first assertRaises passes anyway (PolicyError regardless), but for wrong reason. Change helper to include ios variant in variant_envs but keep config_envs param controlling mismatch. Helper: make_tree(Path(tmp), ["ios-arm64-metal", "linux-x64-vulkan"], steps_toml, config_envs). Then mismatch cases: config [linux-x64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl] vs variants [ios, linux] → mismatch both ways ✓; config [] → mismatch ✓.
- test_prefix_must_match_a_variant passes skip_prefixes ["ios-"] with variants linux only → error ✓ still (variants include ios? no—in that test variants are the default ["linux-x64-vulkan"]... wait the helper hardcodes ["linux-x64-vulkan"]. If I change the helper to include ios-arm64-metal, then test_prefix_must_match_a_variant's prefix "ios-" WOULD match → no error → test fails. Change that test's prefix to something matching nothing like "win-"... but then it doesn't test ios... any prefix not matching: "win-" fine.

Let me restructure: helper `validate(tmp, steps_toml, config_envs)` uses variant_envs = ["ios-arm64-metal", "linux-x64-vulkan"]. 
- test_unknown_variant_reference: skip_variants ["nope"] → unknown ✓.
- test_prefix_must_match_a_variant: use "win-" ✓.

ty errors: `raw` is `Top[dict[Unknown, Unknown]]` from tomllib list. `data.get("steps")` returns Any? tomllib.loads returns dict[str, Any]; .get returns Any; isinstance(raw_steps, list) narrows to list[Any]; iterating gives Any; isinstance(raw, dict) narrows to dict[Unknown, Unknown] (Top). Passing to dict[str, Any] param fails invariance. Fix: annotate helpers to accept `dict[Any, Any]`? Or `Mapping[str, object]`... isinstance narrows to `dict[Unknown, Unknown]`; a parameter of type `dict[str, Any]`... invariance issue. Simplest: change helper signatures to `dict[Any, Any]`. Unknown→Any compatible. Let me use `dict[Any, Any]`.

+++ /app/.mise/ci/generate_workflow.py

         raise PolicyError(f"{context}: '{key}' must be a non-empty string")

-def _str_tuple(data: dict[str, Any], key: str, context: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
+def _str_tuple(data: dict[Any, Any], key: str, context: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
     value = data.get(key, [])

         raise PolicyError(f"{context}: '{key}' must be a list of strings")

Tests — the tail shows a diff fragment suggesting a failure? "2 files reformatted" then ruff check passed. But the unittest tail printed part of a diff... that looks like a unittest failure diff output. Let me rerun unittest to see result. Also ty still has 2 errors: `raw.get` where raw: Top[dict[Unknown, Unknown]]. The `dict[Any, Any]` helper signatures fixed those, but raw.get itself still errors because raw is Top[dict[Unknown,Unknown]] and .get expects Never? That's a ty quirk with Top narrowing. Fix: annotate explicitly after isinstance:

```python
if not isinstance(raw, dict):
    raise ...
table: dict[Any, Any] = dict(raw)  # or cast
```

`dict(raw)` converts — cheap and satisfies ty. Or use `cast(dict[Any, Any], raw)`. cast is cleaner (typing.cast). Let me use typing.cast.

That output is just stdout noise from tests (main() prints). Note "wrote .github/workflows/ci.yml" — one of the tests wrote to the REAL ci.yml! "wrote .github/workflows/ci.yml" appears — with relative_to(REPO_ROOT) printing ".github/workflows/ci.yml"; in patched tests REPO_ROOT is tmp so relative_to(tmp) would fail... unless the patch context works. Hmm wait, in test_write_then_check, print(f"wrote {WORKFLOW_PATH.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)}") — WORKFLOW_PATH is tmp/.github/workflows/ci.yml, REPO_ROOT patched to tmp → relative OK, prints same path. So that line could be from the patched test. But also "is in sync" line. Both from MainTest presumably. But did any test write the real file? test_invalid_policy_fails returns 2 before writing. # Generated by `mise run ci:generate-workflow` from the CI policy manifests in
# .mise/ci/. Edit those manifests and regenerate; do not edit the output by hand.
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene

display.text:
# Generated by `mise run ci:generate-workflow` from the CI policy manifests in
# .mise/ci/. Edit those manifests and regenerate; do not edit the output by hand.
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint
      - run: mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check
      - run: mise exec -- python -m unittest discover -s .mise/ci -t .mise/ci -v

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal' }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

part.text: Fix the eval: replace `&&` → `and`, `||` → `or`.

input.command:
cd /app && python3 - <<'EOF'
import re, subprocess, sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".mise/ci")
import generate_workflow as gw

old = subprocess.run(["git", "show", "HEAD:.github/workflows/ci.yml"], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout

def old_step_applies(cond, env):
    if cond is None: return True
    c = re.sub(r"!startsWith\(matrix\.mise_env, '([^']+)'\)", lambda m: str(not env.startswith(m.group(1))), cond)
    c = re.sub(r"startsWith\(matrix\.mise_env, '([^']+)'\)", lambda m: str(env.startswith(m.group(1))), c)
    c = re.sub(r"contains\(matrix\.mise_env, '([^']+)'\)", lambda m: str(m.group(1) in env), c)
    c = re.sub(r"matrix\.mise_env == '([^']+)'", lambda m: str(env == m.group(1)), c)
    c = re.sub(r"matrix\.mise_env != '([^']+)'", lambda m: str(env != m.group(1)), c)
    return eval(c.replace("&&", " and ").replace("||", " or "), {}, {})

old_steps = re.findall(r"- name: ([^\n]+)\n(?:        if: \$\{\{ (.+?) \}\}\n)?        run: ([^\n]+)", old)
variants = gw.load_variants(gw.CI_DIR)
new_steps = gw.load_steps(gw.CI_DIR)
envs = [v.mise_env for v in variants]
ok = True
for name, cond, run in old_steps:
    new = next((s for s in new_steps if s.name == name), None)
    if new is None or new.run != run:
        print("MISMATCH step:", name); ok = False; continue
    for env in envs:
        if old_step_applies(cond or None, env) != gw.applies_to(new, env):
            print("APPLICABILITY MISMATCH:", name, env); ok = False
old_matrix = dict(re.findall(r"- mise_env: (\S+)\n            runner: (\S+)", old))
new_matrix = {v.mise_env: v.runner for v in variants}
if old_matrix != new_matrix:
    print("MATRIX MISMATCH"); ok = False
print("SEMANTIC EQUIVALENCE:", "OK" if ok else "FAILED")
EOF

state.output:
Project-managed dependency state keeps builds reproducible. Mise supplies
repository tools, owns task execution, and exports the environment consumed by
build systems, bindings, and examples. Pixi currently supplies desktop native
libraries from [`conda-forge`](https://conda-forge.org/). Platform SDKs such as
Xcode, Visual Studio, Linux compiler packages, and the Android SDK remain host
toolchain inputs.

[`mise`](https://mise.jdx.dev/) is the contributor entrypoint. It pins top-level
tools, installs Git hooks, selects backend variants, and runs repository tasks.
Use `mise run ...` for common workflows: build, test, check, fix, and examples.
Mise also provides ecosystem entrypoints such as Pixi, Zig, Python, uv, Node,
and pnpm.

[`pixi`](https://pixi.sh/) installs the desktop native libraries used by local
builds. CMake, Ninja, pkg-config, shader tools, Doxygen, clang-format, and
clang-tidy are pinned by mise. Host platform toolchains provide C and C++
compilers, and CMake builds the native C/C++ library.

Language package managers own dependencies inside their ecosystems. For example,
`uv` owns Python package dependencies, `pnpm` owns Node package dependencies,
Gradle owns Java and Kotlin dependencies, and Cargo owns Rust dependencies.
Language-specific formatters, linters, analyzers, test frameworks, and code
generators usually live with the language package graph they serve.

[`hk`](https://github.com/jdx/hk) orchestrates repository checks for pre-commit,
`mise run check`, and `mise run fix`. [`dprint`](https://dprint.dev/) owns
repository-wide formatting defaults.

[Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/)
build the documentation site. Generated API reference HTML is installed into
`docs/public/reference/` before each docs build.

## Tests And Examples

Every feature needs automated CI coverage when practical. The root
`mise run test` command builds the native library, runs the direct Zig C API

checked-in workflow stays in sync.

part.text:
Now final verification pass:
1. Re-run generator + check + tests once more.
2. Review git status/diff of everything.
3. Commit.

Also double check that `mise.toml` formatting (dprint toml) is okay — my added task block follows existing style. The description line is long (>80 chars) — mise.toml has long lines already (e.g. windows_default_file_shell_args). dprint toml plugin formats TOML — does it wrap long strings? No, TOML plugin doesn't wrap strings. Fine.

Also check hk.pkl — should I add a lint step to enforce drift check on pre-commit? The user said enforce sync — CI hygiene does it. Pre-commit hk runs dprint/actionlint/jvl/vp-check/ruff/ty on changed files. Adding the generator check to hk could be nice (when .mise/ci files change, check ci.yml in sync). That'd be a good campsite addition... but "keep normal contributor workflow simple". The CI hygiene job enforces it. I could add an hk step:

```
["ci-workflow"] {
  glob = List(".mise/ci/**", ".github/workflows/ci.yml")
  check = "mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check"
}
```

Hmm — but hk check runs on changed files matching glob; running generator check then is fast. It would catch drift before push. Is it over-engineering? The requirement: "enforce that it stays in sync". CI enforcement is the must; pre-commit is a bonus that improves the contributor loop. I'll add it — small, aligned with repo patterns.

Wait, but hk `check` with `fix = true` hooks... in pre-commit (fix mode), steps run `fix` if defined else `check`? In hk, if a step has no `fix`, it runs `check` in fix mode? Actually in hk, for fix hooks, steps without a fix command run their check command (I believe check-only steps still run). Could set fix = "mise run ci:generate-workflow" so pre-commit auto-regenerates... but auto-staging generated output could surprise. hk fix steps stage their changes (the pre-commit hook has fix=true, stash=git). Providing fix would regenerate and stage — actually nice: edit manifests → commit → ci.yml regenerated automatically. But it would also stage ci.yml even if user forgot — that's the drift prevention at commit time. Hmm, but if generation output isn't dprint-formatted... it is.

Keep it simple and safe: check-only step (no fix). Drift fails pre-commit with a clear message; contributor runs the documented command.

Let me add that to hk.pkl.
Let me also wire the drift check into the pre-commit hooks (check-only, matching repo patterns), then do a final verification:

part.text:
All checks pass. One more sanity check on hk.pkl syntax — I can't run hk (not installed), but the pkl edit mirrors the existing actionlint block exactly. pkl isn't installed either (`aqua:apple/pkl` failed to install). The block is syntactically identical in shape to neighbors; risk low.

Wait — one issue with the hk step: `check = "mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check"` — hk runs check with `{{files}}` appended? No: if `{{files}}` is not in the command, hk doesn't append files (hk appends files only if the template references them? Actually hk appends files to the command if there's no {{files}} placeholder? Let me recall hk semantics: in hk, if the check command doesn't contain {{files}}, files are NOT appended... Hmm. From hk docs: "check = "prettier --check {{files}}"" typical. If you omit {{files}}, I believe hk runs the command as-is once per batch? Actually hk runs the command once with files appended only when {{files}} present; without placeholder it just runs the command (with files as args? no). Let me check hk docs quickly... From hk configuration docs: steps define `check` command; `{{files}}` is replaced with the files. If not present, the command runs without file args. I believe that's right (e.g., Builtins for some tools run without files). To be safe, I recall hk's docs state: "if {{files}} is not in the command, files will not be appended". Good.

Also `mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check` inside hk: hk runs commands through the shell with mise tools on PATH (hk is mise-installed; when run via pre-commit hook installed by `hk install --mise`, mise is available). ci:lint uses `hk fix --all` within mise run; and hk steps call `uv run`, `pnpm exec`, etc. directly. `mise` itself on PATH inside an hk hook — pre-commit runs in repo shell where mise may or may not be activated. Other steps avoid calling mise... _ktfmt is called via `mise run _ktfmt` inside dprint.jsonc exec config! So calling mise from within hk/dprint context is already an established pattern in this repo. 

Now — the generated-file header says "do not edit the output by hand" — negative wording in prose? AGENTS.md prose rules: negative wording for real prohibitions — this is a real prohibition, fine.

One thing I should double-check: the `docs` job and the overall `dprint` formatting of AGENTS.md/overview.md (markdown textWrap: always). My added lines are <80 chars? Let me check the overview paragraph lines — they look ≤80. AGENTS.md code block lines fine.

Also: dprint markdown wraps text always — my paragraph is manually wrapped at ≤80; consistent.

Also update CONTRIBUTING.md? Let me check if it mentions CI... quick grep.

Move the CI target support policy out of the hand-written workflow into
manifests under .mise/ci/ (one per variant, plus step applicability rules
and a workflow template). ci.yml is now rendered by ci:generate-workflow,
validated against the .mise/config.*.toml variants, and kept in sync by a
hygiene drift check, a pre-commit hk step, and unit tests for the
generator." && git log --oneline -2

part.text: Committed. Summarize for the user.

Committed as `0199847`. Here's what the change does:

## Declarative policy in `.mise/ci/`
- **`variants/*.toml`** — one manifest per CI target (11 total): `mise_env` + GitHub runner, with comments explaining special cases (iOS build-only, `macos-arm64-egl` partial coverage).
- **`steps.toml`** — each variant-job step declared once with `run` plus applicability rules (`skip_variants`, `only_variants`, `skip_prefixes`, `only_prefixes`, `contains`), preserving the Kotlin/Native rationale comments.
- **`workflow.template.yml`** — static workflow structure with two `# @generated:` markers where the matrix and variant steps are rendered.
- **`generate_workflow.py`** — stdlib-only generator that renders `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, and validates the policy: manifests must match the `.mise/config.*.toml` variants 1:1, every rule must reference real variants/prefixes, and no step may apply to zero variants. `--check` is a non-mutating drift check with a diff.
- **`test_generate_workflow.py`** — 16 unit tests covering condition rendering/ordering, manifest validation failures, template rendering, and `--check` exit codes.

## Enforcement
- New `mise run ci:generate-workflow` task (`-- --check` for the drift check), verified arg pass-through.
- The CI **hygiene job** (in the template, so it can't drift away) now runs the drift check and the generator unit tests.
- A check-only **`ci-workflow` hk step** catches drift at pre-commit when manifests or `ci.yml` change.

## Behavior preserved
The generated `ci.yml` is byte-identical to the old workflow except: the generated-file header, the two new hygiene steps, and alphabetical matrix ordering (behavior-neutral — verified semantic equivalence of every step's per-variant applicability and the runner mapping against `HEAD`).

Generator passes `ruff check`, `ruff format --check`, `ty --error-on-warning`, and all tests; AGENTS.md and the docs overview now document the workflow.

One environment note: this sandbox couldn't run `mise` end-to-end (read-only `/opt/mise-data` blocks installing the full toolset), so I verified the generator directly with Python 3.14 and the mise task mechanism in isolation — worth one `mise run ci:generate-workflow -- --check` run on a normal machine.
